title: Enabling Attribute-Based Access Control for Kubernetes is security-sensitive
description: Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) in Kubernetes allows fine-grained access decisions based on user attributes. However, ABAC policies can be complex and error-prone, potentially leading to excessive permissions or privilege escalation. Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) is recommended for most use cases due to its simplicity and auditability.
message: ABAC is enabled for Kubernetes. Prefer RBAC for better security and manageability.
impact: Misconfigured ABAC policies can grant excessive privileges, increasing the risk of unauthorized access or privilege escalation.
recommendation: Disable ABAC and use RBAC for access control in Kubernetes clusters. If ABAC is required, review policies carefully and audit permissions regularly.

noncompliant.description: ABAC is enabled, which can lead to complex and risky access policies.

compliant.description: ABAC is disabled, and RBAC is used for access control.

why_is_this_an_issue: ABAC policies are difficult to audit and maintain, increasing the risk of misconfiguration and privilege escalation.
how_to_fix: Disable ABAC in cluster configuration. Use RBAC for all access control needs. Audit existing policies for excessive permissions.
